'''Kieran Rush''' (born 3 September 2002) is a [[Jamaica national rugby league team|Jamaica international]] [[rugby league]] footballer who plays as a {{rlp|sh}} or {{rlp|hk}} for the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]].

He has spent time on loan from [[Huddersfield Giants|Huddersfield]] at [[Swinton Lions]], [[Rochdale Hornets]] and [[Keighley Cougars]] in [[RFL League One|League One]] and at [[Dewsbury Rams]] in the [[RFL Championship|Championship]].

He is a product of the Giants Academy system.

==Background==
Rush was born in [[Dewsbury]], [[West Yorkshire, England]]. He is of [[Jamaica]]n descent.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.zerotackle.com/players/kieran-rush/ |title=Kieran Rush| website=Zero Tackle |access-date=12 May 2026}}</ref>

==Playing career==
===Club career===
Rush is contracted to the [[Huddersfield Giants]] in the [[Super League]] after progressing through the clubs' Academy systems. He made his Super League Debut for the Giants in round 12 of Super League 2023 against the Leigh Leopards.

===International career===
In 2022 Rush was named in the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup squads#Jamaica|Jamaica squad]] for the [[2021 Men's Rugby League World Cup|2021 Rugby League World Cup]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.loverugbyleague.com/post/rugby-league-world-cup-2021-squad-lists/ |title=Full list of every squad at the Rugby League World Cup 2021 |website=Love Rugby League |date=4 October 2022 |access-date=12 May 2026}}</ref> He made his debut in the halves in the [[2021 Rugby League World Cup Group C|Group C match]] against [[Ireland national rugby league team|Ireland]] at [[Headingley Rugby Stadium]] in [[Leeds, West Yorkshire]].<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/match/EVP3376399 |title=Jamaica 2-48 Ireland |work=BBC Sport |access-date=12 May 2026}}</ref>
Rush made history when he kicked a penalty goal to register Jamaica's first ever points in a Rugby League world cup.<ref>{{cite web|url=https://www.bbc.co.uk/sport/rugby-league/63277983 |title=Rugby League World Cup: Ireland 48-2 Jamaica - Wolfhounds score 10 tries in emphatic win |work=BBC Sport |date=16 October 2022 |access-date=12 May 2026}}</ref>
